title: lathyrus nissolia var. linearis
intro: Lathyrus nissolia var. linearis, also known as the Narrow-stemmed Sweet Pea, is a perennial legume species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its rich content of protein, fiber, and saponins. It is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Lathyrus nissolia var. linearis is often used as a tea or tincture, or added to soups and salads, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption may cause lathyrism due to the presence of a toxin called ODAP.
